ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ŋɔ u be lugɔ u balii Jekɔb kunaa u kpa di ra ba ú wa tɛɛ u dɔɔri Ɛsau. U haa du harbɔɔ di ra ba.”
translated: And Jacob said to Esau, 'I will give you a gift, I will give you a present, so that you may accept me, and that you may bless me.' And Esau said, 'What is it that you have?'

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Ɛsau haa vɛɛ suŋŋuu, di ŋɔ, “Amɔ, kissikpaŋ bɛnnɛ i dɔɔ di ra kissi nɛraa? N mɛ, kissi n ge gba!” Uŋ wa ŋɔ guŋ tuŋ, u ra chɛɛhgi.
translated: Esau said to Jacob, “Am I not more deserving of your father’s blessing than you? I am the older brother! Give me the blessing!” Jacob was angry with him, and he was afraid.

ref: After this, they took the coat to their father and said, “We found this! Look at it carefully and see if it belongs to your son.”
text: ka jaa kpau di la ba mɛɛ be, di ga ŋɔ, “Pir biinii yaa na tagtajaŋ naa. Pessuu na, di Joosɛf tagtaarɛ gɛɛ waa lɛiyɛi?”
translated: When I went to the market, I saw a man and asked him, “Is this the place where they sell the best things? I want to buy Joseph’s goods, where are they?”

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: I ni a haaŋ daŋ dɔm, ií biizi ge dɔm. U bie daŋ chanzi i nyuu, i ge dum u naalume.”
translated: If you are going to speak, speak the truth. Do not speak lies. If you are going to tell a story, tell the truth.

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: Laban pɛɛzuu, “N turgii aŋmɛnɛi?” Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Gilɛɛ tɛŋ kummaa. Ama, di i daŋ ɛɛ wikpaŋ naa tɛŋ, n daŋ haa ra binni i sela ni.
translated: Laban said, “Where are you going?” Jacob replied, “I am going to a distant place. I will not stay here any longer, for I must go to my own land.”

ref: just as God had told him to do. And when they were all in the boat, the Lord closed the door.
text: gunla himmaa Kɔraawiizi n ŋɔwɛɛ la. Denii Kɔraawiizi faa tɔ a muugberzeŋ dominua.
translated: The gun is for Koraawiizi, and he is going to use it. Koraawiizi will use it to fight.

ref: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
text: Ba faa du a muugberzeŋ biinii yawa bir, ka a nii haa ba di wa su a hɛri maa.
translated: If you want to go to the market, you should go early in the morning because it is crowded in the afternoon.

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Laban sɛɛ di ŋɔ, “Miŋ kpau tɛi bɔmbɔ, miŋ kpau tɛɛ nɛbɛl. Awia, so n be ɛŋ.”
translated: I will not be a fool, I will not be a slave.  Go, so that you may be.

ref: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
text: u yir u diahina, di ŋɔ, “Ií na gunla Hibru hina yombaal naa n jaari n yauwɛ naa n ɔɔ hanna binni yaa pɛɛ, haali u wa zuu ŋɔ ú pumiŋ, ka n lɔ wile werge maa.
translated: You are a divine being, oh, “I am the God of the Hebrews, I have heard your cry and I have seen your suffering. Now, go and tell Pharaoh to let my people go.”

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Baŋ faa wa lii de la, Kɔraawiizi gil ɔnsii werge lau nɛra la n du bola la maa n bar baa la, ba faa waa sɛɛwɛ bá duŋŋi ba luge.
translated: When they arrived at the place, Korawizi said to them, "I will give you a gift, but you must first tell me what you want."
